title: Metagenomic sequencing protocol for respiratory virus detection and sequencing from negative SARS-CoV-2 rapid antigen tests
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1mgbb","text":"This protocol outlines methods for RNA metagenomic sequencing to identify and characterize viruses, which we apply here to nasopharyngeal swab samples obtained from negative SARS-CoV-2 rapid antigen tests. Steps include: RNA extraction, carrier RNA depletion, cDNA synthesis (Superscript IV, ThermoFisher), and library tagmentation/indexing/amplification (Nextera XT, Illumina). Methods are based on doi: 10.3791/54117 and doi: 10.1128/mBio.01143-21 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"95k1o","text":"To minimize environmental contamination, three separate areas of the laboratory are used to: 1) prepare reagents, 2) handle samples and pre-amplified material, and 3) handle amplified material. There is a unidirectional workflow, with one individual working exclusively in the reagent area and another individual working with samples. In the protocol below, all steps are performed in the reagent area, except when noted as “sample area” or “post-amplification area\".","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare each of the following reagent mixes separately. (These volumes are sufficient for up to 40 RNA samples including any controls. If more samples are processed, increase the volume proportionately.)
Linear Acrylamide (LA) Water - make this first %s %s
1 M Tris-HCl pH 7.5 (1000 µl) - make this second %s %s
Prepare Hybridization Mix, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s
5X Hybridization Buffer (100 µl) %s %s
Prepare RNAClean Reagents: %s %s
10X RNase H Reaction Buffer (100 µl) %s %s

Prepare Tagmentation Reagent Mix (on ice block), multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s Additionally, prepare an aliquot of NT buffer at room temperature, multiplying the volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s Then, prepare a mix of Nextera PCR Reagent Mix and water, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s Lastly, prepare DNAClean Reagents: %s %s
Prepare the RNase Mix and distribute into a strip tube, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s
Sample Area: %s %s
Cycling Conditions: %s %s
Sample Area: %s %s
Prepare DNAse Mix, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s
1. Sample Area: %s %s
RNA SPRI Procedure: %s %s
Random Priming: Aliquot random primers, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%. Vortex and spin. %s %s
Sample Area: %s %s
First Strand Synthesis Mix: Make First Strand Synthesis Mix, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%.Vortex and spin: %s %s
Sample Area: %s %s Cycling Conditions: %s %s
Second Strand Synthesis Mix: Make Second Strand Synthesis Mix, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%. Vortex and spin: %s %s
Sample Area: %s %s Cycling Conditions: %s %s

Prepare DNAClean Reagents: A B 1 DNA SPRI Beads 153 µl per sample 2 71% EtOH 7.1 mL 100% EtOH + 2.9 mL nuclease-free water 3 EDTA 5 µl per sample 4 Elution Buffer (EB) 1:100 ratio of Tris pH 8 to nuclease free water Sample Area: DNA SPRI Procedure: %s %s

Sample Area: %s %s Tagmentation Cycling Conditions: %s %s Nextera Cycling Conditions: %s %s DNA SPRI Procedure: A B C D 1 DNA SPRI Procedure vol. (µl) Wait time (minutes) Notes 2 1) Add 0.7X SPRI Beads, MIX WELL 42 10 mix halfway through 3 2) Place on magnet 5 4 3) Remove buffer 5 4)71% EtOH wash 150 1 6 5) Remove EtOH 7 6) 71% EtOH Wash 150 1 8 7) Remove EtOH, dry sample 10 wait until beads no longer appear glossy, but instead look matte 9 8) Resuspend beads in EB, MIX WELL 11 10 mix halfway through 10 9) Place on magnet 5 11 10) Transfer 10 µl eluent to a screw cap tube and store at -20°C until sequencing or pooling.

This extraction protocol uses the Qiagen EZ1 Advanced XL Instrument and Qiagen EZ1 DSP Virus Kit: https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/diagnostics-and-clinical-research/solutions-for-laboratory-developed-tests/ez1-dsp-virus-kit-na Add Linear Acrylamide to the AVE Buffer, multiplying each volume of reagent by the number of samples and overage volume of 15%: %s %s Extraction Protocol: A 1 1. Label 2.0 mL unskirted conical-bottom sample tubes. Add 250 uL of Qiagen Buffer G2 Digestion buffer to each tube. 2 2. Remove the swab from the BinaxNow cartridge. Swirl it in buffer for 10 secs. Squeeze the swab on the side of the sample tube while removing the swab from the buffer. 3 3. Wipe the outside of the instrument with 70% EtOH, and insert the EZ1 Advanced XL DSP Virus Card into the card slot. 4 4. Turn on instrument and run the cleaning operation. Clean the piercing unit with 70% EtOH. 5 5. Press START to start work table setup. Select 200 uL sample and 60 uL elution volumes. 6 6. Insert loaded cartridge rack into the EZ1 instrument: a. Invert reagent cartridges (RCV) 3 times and tap the cartridges to deposit the reagents. Slide 1 cartridge rack per sample into the cartridge rack until it clicks into place. b. Load empty 2.0 mL unskirted conical bottom tubes into the heating system slot of each cartridge. 7 7. Insert loaded tip rack into the EZ1 instrument: a. First row: 1.5 mL conical-bottom elution tubes b. Second row: Tip holders containing tips c. Third row: 1.5 mL conical bottom tube containing linear acrylamide and AVE buffer solution d. Fourth row: Samples from Step 2 8 8. Close the instrument door and press START to run EZ1 Virus Protocol. 9 9. Aliquot 30uL from the elution tubes into a new 1.5 mL conical bottom tube.

KAPA Quantification: Thaw the KAPA reaction mix and qPCR standards. Standard concentrations are as follows: %s %s Prepare the KAPA qPCR mix in a 1.5 ml tube on an ice block as listed below, multiplying the volume of each reagent by the number of samples, including the 6 standards and 1 negative control, and overage volume of 15%. Vortex and spin down. %s %s Add 12.4 µl of mix to each well in an optical 96-well plate on an ice block, and then 8 µl of the NTC and 6 standards to the appropriate wells. Sample Area: Make dilutions of the libraries with EB, and then water. Make a 1:100 dilution with EB, then make a further 1:100 dilution with water (final dilution will be 1:10,000). Add 8 µl of the final 1:10,000 dilution of each sample to the appropriate well. Seal plate, gently vortex, and spin down. Run the plate under the following cycling conditions: %s %s

Pooling: After KAPA qPCR, note the respective concentrations of each sample in nM. Normalize each sample to the desired concentration (typically 4 nM based on Illumina calculations). Make the necessary dilutions to each sample, pooling the libraries to equimolar concentrations. If the total volume of the pool is too low, the total pool volume may be brought up to 50 µl with EB. Then perform a 0.8X SPRI. Sample Area: Perform DNA SPRI Procedure: Warm SPRI beads to room temperature (30 min) before using. %s %s To quantify the clean pool, perform another KAPA qPCR as outlined in Step 11 go to step #12 , making the appropriate dilutions. Once the pool has been quantified, proceed to sequencing and prepare the pool according to manufacturer's instructions of the respective sequencing kit.
